mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate, directly boosting the present value of equity cash flows and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses below 6.5%, labor market tightness accelerates wage growth and consumer spending, which lifts corporate earnings expectations and pushes S&P 500 returns higher over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day forward returns in more than 50% of instances where UNRATE drops below 6.5% within a 3-month window.

mechanism_narrative: Strong industrial production growth combined with rising residential housing starts signals robust aggregate demand outpacing supply constraints, leading to margin expansion and positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising INDPRO and HOUST fails to predict positive 60-day forward returns in more than 60% of out-of-sample windows.

mechanism_narrative: In a zero-bound rate environment, increases in real consumer credit balances directly fuel equity risk-taking and leverage, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as liquidity floods into risk assets.
failure_condition: Positive RSAFS growth under ZIRP conditions correlates with negative or flat 5-day SPX returns in more than 55% of rolling 12-month periods.

mechanism_narrative: Sequential quarterly growth in real GDP expands the earnings base for S&P 500 constituents, mechanically driving positive forward equity returns over a 60-day horizon as corporate profit margins stabilize.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDPC1 growth rates show no statistically significant correlation (p > 0.10) with subsequent 60-day SPX returns over a 10-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: When the CPI index crosses above 260.0, inflation expectations become embedded in pricing models, compressing equity valuations and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns remain positive in more than 65% of periods following CPIAUCSL breaches of 260.0.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid payroll growth paired with declining unemployment indicates a self-reinforcing labor demand shock that boosts household income and consumption, lifting equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: The combined signal of rising PAYEMS and falling UNRATE fails to predict positive 5-day forward returns in more than 50% of out-of-sample windows.

mechanism_narrative: During supply chain stress periods, a divergence where industrial production growth outpaces housing starts signals manufacturing-led recovery rather than construction-led demand, which historically supports cyclical equity outperformance over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: The INDPRO-HOUST divergence signal predicts negative 20-day forward returns in more than 60% of instances during supply-constrained periods.
